methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $7.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $250.0B.

data.narrative:
Groq, a Series E AI & Software company founded by Jonathan Ross, is valued at $7.0B post-money following $3.89B in total capital raised. The valuation analysis benchmarks against a peer set of 9 companies (1 public, 8 private) at the same stage, including xAI, Crusoe Energy, SandboxAQ, Ayar Labs, and WEKA.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression methodology, anchoring to the $7.0B last post-money valuation and applying a typical 1.4x step-up multiplier for Series E progression, implies $9.8B. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $4.7B, though the peer set exhibits substantial dispersion (range: $1.1B to $250.0B), reflecting heterogeneous business scale and investor perception within frontier AI.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $4.0B (low) to $11.3B (high), with a median of $7.2B. The current $7.0B valuation aligns closely with this median, suggesting fair pricing relative to stage peers. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in comparable valuations—particularly xAI's $250.0B outlier—and the early-stage, revenue-light nature of frontier AI companies, which limits traditional financial metrics. Investor confidence in the founding team and technical differentiation materially influences positioning within the derived range.
